content.claim: Parental and school choice in Finland enables selective sorting by prior student performance, with 'capable' (typically higher-SES) parents selecting into selective classes, leading to performance-based school fragmentation and concentration of disadvantage in non-selective schools.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Study documents selective enrollment patterns but does not isolate whether choice mechanisms or residential patterns are the primary segregation driver
Long-term outcome effects on segregated cohorts not specified in cited sources
Findings apply to urban context (Helsinki, Turku) and may not generalize to rural/small-town school choice dynamics
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
